channel.title: Denali Tech Blog
channel.description: Smart home, AV, network, lighting, camera, and support guides from Denali Tech.

item.title: WattBox and OvrC Remote Power Reset: Why Smart Homes Need Serviceable Power
item.description: A homeowner guide to WattBox power management, OvrC remote resets, rack planning, UPS choices, and why reliable smart homes need controllable power.

item.title: WattBox vs a Basic Surge Protector: What Homeowners Should Know
item.description: Understand the difference between basic surge protection and managed power for smart homes, theater racks, network gear, TVs, and support after installation.

item.title: Binary Video Distribution: When HDMI, Fiber or AV-over-IP Makes Sense
item.description: A practical guide to Binary HDMI, fiber, matrix switching, AV-over-IP, cable planning, and distributed video decisions for Chicago-area homes.

item.title: Strong AV Racks and Mounts: Why the Rack Shape Matters
item.description: A homeowner guide to AV racks, wall cabinets, ventilation, service access, TV backing, cable paths, and why clean mounting makes systems easier to support.

item.title: SunBrite Outdoor TV Planning: Weather, Glare, Sound and Service Access
item.description: Plan an outdoor TV system with weather-rated displays, sound, WiFi, power, mounting, shade, and service access for real Chicago-area conditions.

item.title: Wirepath Structured Wiring: The Prewire Decisions That Protect a Smart Home
item.description: A practical structured wiring guide for Ethernet, speaker wire, camera wire, TV locations, low-voltage panels, labels, conduit, and future service.

item.title: Control4, Araknis, WattBox, Luma, Binary and Episode: How the Smart Home Stack Fits Together
item.description: A plain-English guide to the Snap One-style smart home stack: control, network, power, cameras, video distribution, audio, racks, and remote support.

item.title: Control4 Scenes and Routines: How a Smart Home Should Feel Day to Day
item.description: A practical homeowner guide to Control4 scenes, routines, room control, and why smart home automation should feel calm instead of complicated.

item.title: Control4 and Apple HomeKit: What Homeowners Should Understand
item.description: Learn how to think about Control4, Apple HomeKit, phones, tablets, and voice control without turning your smart home into a pile of competing apps.

item.title: Smart Home Takeover Service: When an Existing System Needs a Reset
item.description: A homeowner guide to taking over an existing Control4, URC, lighting, AV, WiFi, or theater system without tearing everything out first.

item.title: Araknis Access Point Placement: How Reliable Smart Home WiFi Gets Designed
item.description: A practical guide to access point placement, wiring, coverage, and why serious smart homes need planned WiFi instead of random boosters.

item.title: Outdoor TV, Speakers and WiFi for Chicago Backyards: What to Plan First
item.description: Plan a reliable outdoor entertainment system with weather-rated displays, speakers, WiFi, wiring, power, lighting, and control that can survive real Chicago use.

item.title: Triad Home Theater Speaker Planning: What to Decide Before the Room Is Built
item.description: A plain-English guide to theater speaker planning, room layout, screen placement, subwoofers, in-wall speakers, and why design choices should happen early.

item.title: Episode Speakers and Whole-Home Audio: What to Plan Before Cutting Holes
item.description: A homeowner guide to distributed audio zones, speaker placement, volume control, outdoor audio, and whole-home music planning.

item.title: Smart Home Prewire Checklist for Builders and Remodels
item.description: A practical prewire checklist for builders, remodels, and homeowners planning smart lighting, shades, WiFi, cameras, audio, theater, and future service.

item.title: Smart Home Planning for Designers and Architects: Keep Technology Clean
item.description: How designers and architects can plan smart lighting, shades, AV, speakers, WiFi, cameras, and controls without cluttering the finished space.

item.title: Luma Security Camera Planning: What Homeowners Should Decide Before Installation
item.description: Plan a cleaner camera system by deciding camera goals, viewing angles, recorder location, network wiring, app access, and privacy before installation starts.

item.title: Luxul Epic 5 Router and PoE Switch Setup for a Reliable Smart Home Network
item.description: See how a clean Luxul router and PoE switch install improves reliability for Wi-Fi, cameras, AV, and smart home control systems.

item.title: Motorized Shades with Lutron Pico Control: What a Clean Smart Shades Setup Looks Like
item.description: A practical look at how motorized shades and Lutron Pico control fit into a polished smart home installation.

item.title: AV Rack Wire Management Done Right: What Clean Rack Builds Actually Require
item.description: Clean AV rack wire management is about reliability and serviceability, not just looks. This guide breaks down what matters in a professional rack build.

item.title: 190-Inch Motorized Projector Screen Installation: What Goes Into a Serious Theater Build
item.description: A large motorized projector screen install requires more than hanging a screen. This post covers planning, mounting, control, and theater integration.

item.title: 18U Middle Atlantic AV Rack Build Time-Lapse: What a Clean Rack Installation Should Look Like
item.description: This 18U Middle Atlantic rack build shows how planning, cable discipline, and proper layout create a cleaner and more serviceable AV system.

item.title: SANUS CFR2127 AV Rack Guide: What a Serviceable Home Theater Rack Should Look Like
item.description: This SANUS CFR2127 rack walkthrough shows what separates a clean, serviceable theater rack from a pile of expensive gear with no long-term plan.

item.title: Salamander Oslo 617 with McIntosh MC255: How to Build a Clean High-End AV Cabinet
item.description: A high-end AV cabinet should handle ventilation, cable routing, and component layout without compromising the room. This Salamander Oslo build shows how.

item.title: McIntosh and Bowers & Wilkins Listening Room Guide: What high-end listening rooms Require
item.description: This high-end listening room shows what high-end listening rooms demand: system matching, clean installation, careful layout, and equipment that belongs together.

item.title: McIntosh MC452 in a Salamander Cabinet: What a Proper Hi-Fi Cabinet Install Needs
item.description: A large amplifier install inside furniture only works when heat, cable management, and component spacing are handled correctly. This MC452 setup is a clear example.

item.title: SANUS 15U Mini AV Rack Guide: How Compact Theater Racks Stay Clean and Reliable
item.description: Compact racks need the same discipline as larger builds. This SANUS 15U example shows how to keep a smaller theater rack organized, cool, and serviceable.

item.title: Plex Media Server Build Guide: What a Reliable Home Media Server Actually Needs
item.description: A Plex media server only works well when storage, airflow, power, and system layout are planned correctly. This build shows what that looks like in practice.

item.title: Best Smart Home Brands We Trust for Chicago Homes
item.description: See the smart home brands Denali Tech trusts for Chicago-area homes, including Control4, URC, Lutron, Sonos, Araknis, OvrC, WattBox, and more.

item.title: What Is OvrC? Why Remote Monitoring Matters for Smart Homes
item.description: Learn what OvrC is, how remote monitoring helps smart homes, and why professionally installed systems are easier to support, maintain, and fix when problems happen.
